titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Cadmus Papers

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 22, 2022 at 03:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Series IV: Access temporarily restricted. As of March 2022, these materials are closed pending rehousing. For further information consult Access Services.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Cadmus Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, artwork, printed material, photographs, and other papers by or relating to Paul Cadmus.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Cadmus Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the auhors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven series: I: Correspondence, 1920-2009. II: Personal papers, 1812-2018. III: Paul Cadmus files, 1930-2012. IV: Photographs, 1890-1999. V: Papers of others, 1890-2019. VI: Printed matter, 1930-2009. VII: Artwork, undated.

did.unittitle: Correspondence written by Cadmus with related materials

Address books, appointment books, and other personal papers--Jon F. Anderson

Blueprints for underground installation in Weston, CT home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Donald Windham, George Platt Lynes, Glenway Wescott, Bridget Chisholm, and others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
